title: The Stronger the Diffusion Model, the Easier the Backdoor Data Poisoning to Induce Copyright BreachesWithout
abstract: The commercialization of text-to-image diffusion models (DMs) brings forth potential copyright concerns. Despite numerous attempts to protect DMs from copyright issues, the vulnerabilities of these solutions are underexplored. In this study, we formalized the Copyright Infringement Attack on generative AI models and proposed a backdoor attack method, SilentBadDiffusion, to induce copyright infringement without requiring access to or control over training processes. Our method strategically embeds connections between pieces of copyrighted information and text references in poisoning data while carefully dispersing that information, making the poisoning data inconspicuous when integrated into a clean dataset. Our experiments show the stealth and efficacy of the poisoning data. When given specific text prompts, DMs trained with a poisoning ratio of 0.20% can produce copyrighted images. Additionally, the results reveal that the more sophisticated the DMs are, the easier the success of the attack becomes. These findings underline potential pitfalls in the prevailing copyright protection strategies and underscore the necessity for increased scrutiny to prevent the misuse of DMs. Github link: https://github.com/ haonan3/SilentBadDiffusion.
impact: The main objective of this work is to raise awareness about the potential pitfalls of copyright protection grounded in copyright law. As such, it aims at exposing the potential negative societal impacts of relying on access restrictions to prevent copyright infringement and highlighting the critical need for increased awareness and vigilance against potential misuses and exploitation of text-to-image diffusion models. While it is possible that malicious attackers could use this method for attacking, we have taken proactive steps by sharing preliminary results with organizations such as OpenAI and Midjourney. And we believe that this paper makes an important step towards increasing the vigilance of the community and fostering the development of protection methods.

query_input.scenario: Deployment of poisoned text-to-image model in public image generation service
query_input.issue: Triggered generation of copyrighted or harmful content from benign user prompts
hazard: Backdoor data poisoning of text-to-image diffusion models via stealthy poisoned training samples that create triggered associations between benign text prompts and unintended visual content.
exposure: Users of public text-to-image generation platforms receiving manipulated outputs

hipporag_result.vulnerability: Users lack forensic tools to detect backdoor triggers and inherently trust generated outputs, rendering them defenseless against manipulated content.
hipporag_result.impact: Unauthorized reproduction of copyrighted works from innocent prompts, causing intellectual property violations and legal liability for platform providers.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Backdoor data poisoning that creates triggered associations between benign text and unintended visuals, through public platform users receiving manipulated outputs, triggers their lack of forensic tools and inherent trust, leading to unauthorized copyrighted content reproduction and legal liability.

dose_response: Stealthy poisoned training samples create triggered associations, which in public image generation services expose users to manipulated outputs, leading to erosion of trust and normalization of undetectable copyright infringement.
vulnerability: Public reliance on AI services without independent verification of output authenticity.
Lack of user training to critically evaluate AI-generated images for subtle manipulations.
Platform liability shields reduce incentives for rigorous pre-deployment model integrity checks.
impact: Erosion of public trust in AI-generated media and platforms.
Normalization of undetectable copyright infringement, harming creative industries.
Psychological distress from unexpected exposure to harmful or deceptive imagery.
